free_text: Energy spectra and angular distribution of

Radiochemistry and Nuclear Physics Divisions.
School of Studies in Physics
Self-supporting thulium metal targets, having thickness
around 1 mg/cm2
BARC-TIFR Pelletron Accelerator facility
12C beam of energy 84 MeV.

SD: SF1=SF4=Tm-168->Tm-169 in REACTION code
- By compiler. Cross sections are to be zero for boron
energies < 35.56 MeV and >75.912 MeV.
The kinetic-energy spectrum of boron were
calculated in the framework of the diffractional model
for surface transfer reactions based on the DWBA
formalism of Mermaz using the code fast.
Code FAST
SD: SF1=Tm-168 -> Tm-169 in REACTION code
- By compiler. No events were detected for beryllium
energies < 21.7665 MeV and >77.156 MeV.
Authors' data units: "counts".
The kinetic-energy spectrum of boron were
calculated in the framework of the diffractional model
for surface transfer reactions based on the DWBA
formalism of Mermaz using the code fast.
Code FAST
SD: SF1=Tm-168 -> Tm-169 in REACTION code
Angular distribution were calculated in the
framework of the diffractional model for surface
transfer reactions based on the DWBA formalism using
the code FAST.
Code FAST
Any analysis of data uncertainties is not
given.
SD: SF1=Tm-168 -> Tm-169 in REACTION code
Oxygen contribution.
Complete fusion contribution.
-By authors.  Incomplete fusion give main deposit at
high energy of alpha-particles and 40 degree. Complete
fusion contribution is very low at the conditions.
Complete fusion give main deposit at 130 degrees, but
incomplete fusion contribution is very low at the
conditions.
